steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the lice evidence, if correctly dated to 170,000 years ago, would support. The key detail: clothing-exclusive lice need large populations of clothed humans to survive — so their presence at 170Ka means regular clothing use was already established then. Compare that date to when clothing became thermally necessary (70Ka, after humans left Africa).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'humans needed clothing before leaving Africa': The passage says Africa's climate made clothing unnecessary — this reverses the logic. ✗
  • Choice B — 'non-modern hominids wore clothing': The lice date to early modern humans, not other hominid species. ✗
  • Choice C — 'humans wore clothing long before thermal necessity required it': 170Ka predates the 70Ka out-of-Africa migration by 100,000 years, meaning clothing existed well before survival demanded it. ✓
  • Choice D — 'post-70Ka climate drove early clothing adoption': The lice evidence precedes 70Ka by a wide margin, so cold climate cannot explain the origin. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
